source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - BS 55 EB Platform Allision

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Breton Sound Block 55

actions_taken: Remote shutdown of flowing well EB-12
event.summary: On 13 October 2020 at 0730 hrs, the shrimp boat Master Paul (86 feet long) collided with the upper deck of the unmanned BS 55 EB Platform in Breton Sound Block 55, Gulf of Mexico. The vessel's outrigging contacted the platform structure, causing extensive damage including a broken starboard boom, damaged extended walkway, foghorn, navigation aid light, and davit support. The only flowing well (EB-12) was remotely shut in and secured. No injuries or pollution resulted. Total damage assessed at $125,000. Platform returned to production on 15 October 2020.

hazards.name: Vessel Traffic in Proximity to Platform

threats.name: Shrimp boat operator error - loss of vessel control or navigation awareness
Failure of navigation aids to warn vessel of platform location

consequences.name: Damage to safety systems and navigation equipment
Potential loss of containment from damaged well

controls.name: Navigation Aids (lights and foghorn)
controls.barrier_role: Warning system to alert vessel operators of platform presence
controls.lod_basis: Primary warning mechanism for vessel navigation safety

supporting_text: There were damages to the extended walkway, foghorn, one (1) Aids to Navigation (Nav-aid) light
As a result of damages received to the affected Nav-aid light and foghorn, BSEE approved a variance for the installation of temporary Nav-aids

controls.name: Remote Well Shutdown System
controls.barrier_role: Isolate production to prevent loss of containment following structural damage
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to mitigate consequences of structural damage

supporting_text: Based on the assessment of the damages to the upper structure, it was relayed to the lead operator at MP 42 M to remotely shut in and secure the only flowing well: EB-12 well
The production foreman witnessed the SSV (Surface Safety Valve) shut on the well and the well remotely secured

controls.name: Standby Vessel Monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Observation and early detection of vessel activity near platform
controls.lod_basis: Early warning system to detect vessel approach

supporting_text: the production foreman looked out the window at the MP 41 field and noticed a trawling vessel next to BS 55 EB structure
he instructed the Motor Vessel (M/V) Ms Dee, standing by the MP 42 C structure, to travel to BS 55 EB to observe the situation

controls.name: Unmanned Platform Design
controls.barrier_role: Eliminate personnel exposure to structural damage consequences
controls.lod_basis: Reduces personnel injury risk from structural damage

supporting_text: BS 55 EB is an unmanned, fixed leg, satellite platform operated by Cantium, LLC
There were no injuries or pollution associated with this allision

controls.name: Platform Assessment and Safe Boarding Procedures
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel injury during damage assessment and recovery operations
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to ensure safe operations following incident

supporting_text: Based on the assessment of the damages to the upper structure, it was relayed to the lead operator at MP 42 M to remotely shut in and secure the only flowing well
Due to the damages seen, they did not board the platform

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
